messages.content:
what does below R software code try to tell?

remove(list=ls())

# Load the required package
library(survival)
set.seed(42)

# Generate the sample dataset
nPatients <- 200
status <- rbinom(nPatients, 1, 0.7)
gender <- rbinom(nPatients, 1, 0.5)
age <- rnorm(nPatients, mean = 60, sd = 10)
time <- rexp(nPatients, rate = 0.015)

simData <- data.frame(time = time, status = status, gender = gender, age = age)

# Fit the Cox proportional hazards model
cph_model_updated <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ gender + age, data = simData)

# Get the data for alive patients only
alive_data <- simData[simData[["status"]] == 0, ]

# Calculate linear predictions for alive patients
linear_pred_updated <- predict(cph_model_updated, newdata = alive_data)

length(linear_pred_updated)
# Estimate survival probabilities for alive patients
surv_prob_all_updated <- survfit(cph_model_updated)


summary(surv_prob_all_updated)


estimated_surv_probs_updated <- list()
for(i in 1:length(linear_pred_updated)) {
  lp <- linear_pred_updated[i] 
  patient_current_time <- alive_data[i, "time"]
  patient_times_start_index <- which.min(abs(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]] - patient_current_time))
  patient_surv_probs <- surv_prob_all_updated[["surv"]][patient_times_start_index:length(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]])] * exp(lp)
  estimated_surv_probs_updated[[i]] <- patient_surv_probs
}


weighted_avg_time <- function(surv_probs, surv_times) {
  sum(surv_probs * surv_times) / sum(surv_probs)
}

# Calculate adjusted survival probabilities for each alive patient
conditional_surv_probs_single_point <- lapply(1:length(alive_data), function(i) {
  patient_current_time <- alive_data[i, "time"]
  patient_times_start_index <- which.min(abs(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]] - patient_current_time))
  patient_surv_probs_cumulative <- estimated_surv_probs_updated[[i]]
  
  # Calculate conditional single time point survival probabilities
  patient_surv_probs_single_point <- c(1, diff(patient_surv_probs_cumulative) / head(patient_surv_probs_cumulative, -1))
  patient_surv_probs_single_point
})

# Calculate additional survival time estimates based on conditional single time point survival probabilities
additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional <- data.frame(PatientID = rownames(alive_data))
wa_median_times_updated_conditional <- numeric(nrow(alive_data))
for (i in 1:nrow(alive_data)) {
  start_time_index <- which.min(abs(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]] - alive_data[i, "time"]))
  wa_median_times_updated_conditional[i] <- weighted_avg_time(conditional_surv_probs_single_point[[i]], surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]][start_time_index:length(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]])])
}
additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional$WeightedAverageSurvivalTime <- wa_median_times_updated_conditional - alive_data[["time"]]

# View the updated additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional
additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional

The R code aims to create a simulation to estimate the additional survival time for patients using the Cox Proportional Hazards model. Here's a step-by-step description of the code:

1. It begins by removing any objects in the workspace using `remove(list=ls())`.
2. It loads the required package "survival" for survival analysis.
3. It sets a seed using `set.seed(42)` for reproducible results.
4. It generates a sample dataset for a hypothetical group of 200 patients with variables such as status (alive or dead), gender, age, and time (duration of the study).
5. It fits the Cox Proportional Hazards model using the variables gender and age as predictors.
6. It updates the dataset by keeping only the rows with alive patients.
7. It calculates the linear predictions for alive patients using the updated Cox Proportional Hazards model.
8. It estimates survival probabilities for alive patients based on the fitted model.
9. It creates and calculates a weighted average function for the survival time.
10. For each alive patient, it calculates their conditional single time point survival probabilities and additional survival time based on their current state and the current time.
11. It creates a data frame with the updated survival time estimates for each alive patient.

To summarize, this R code simulates a dataset, fits a survival model, estimates survival probabilities, and computes additional survival time for patients.

below error is reported when running the code, can you please help fix?

> for (i in 1:nrow(alive_data)) {
+   start_time_index <- which.min(abs(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]] - alive_data[i, "time"]))
+   wa_median_times_updated_conditional[i] <- weighted_avg_time(conditional_surv_probs_single_point[[i]], surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]][start_time_index:length(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]])])
+ }
Error in conditional_surv_probs_single_point[[i]] : 
  subscript out of bounds

It seems that there is an issue with the `conditional_surv_probs_single_point` list while accessing it with index `i`. Based on the code, the `conditional_surv_probs_single_point` list should have the same length as `alive_data`. So, let's print the length of `alive_data` and the length of the `conditional_surv_probs_single_point` list to check if these are the same. If they are not, that's likely the cause of the "subscript out of bounds" error.

Add these two lines before the problematic loop:

```R
print(length(alive_data))
print(length(conditional_surv_probs_single_point))
```

If the lengths are not the same, then the issue is happening during the calculation of `conditional_surv_probs_single_point`. In this case, you can debug the loop that builds that list and make sure it has the same length as the `alive_data`.

Please let me know the output of the print statements, and I can help you further.

Here are the full code, please help fix:

# Load the required package
library(survival)
set.seed(42)

# Generate the sample dataset
nPatients <- 200
status <- rbinom(nPatients, 1, 0.7)
gender <- rbinom(nPatients, 1, 0.5)
age <- rnorm(nPatients, mean = 60, sd = 10)
time <- rexp(nPatients, rate = 0.015)

simData <- data.frame(time = time, status = status, gender = gender, age = age)

# Fit the Cox proportional hazards model
cph_model_updated <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ gender + age, data = simData)

# Get the data for alive patients only
alive_data <- simData[simData[["status"]] == 0, ]

# Calculate linear predictions for alive patients
linear_pred_updated <- predict(cph_model_updated, newdata = alive_data)

length(linear_pred_updated)
# Estimate survival probabilities for alive patients
surv_prob_all_updated <- survfit(cph_model_updated)


summary(surv_prob_all_updated)


estimated_surv_probs_updated <- list()
for(i in 1:length(linear_pred_updated)) {
  lp <- linear_pred_updated[i] 
  patient_current_time <- alive_data[i, "time"]
  patient_times_start_index <- which.min(abs(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]] - patient_current_time))
  patient_surv_probs <- surv_prob_all_updated[["surv"]][patient_times_start_index:length(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]])] * exp(lp)
  estimated_surv_probs_updated[[i]] <- patient_surv_probs
}


weighted_avg_time <- function(surv_probs, surv_times) {
  sum(surv_probs * surv_times) / sum(surv_probs)
}

# Calculate adjusted survival probabilities for each alive patient
conditional_surv_probs_single_point <- lapply(1:length(alive_data), function(i) {
  patient_current_time <- alive_data[i, "time"]
  patient_times_start_index <- which.min(abs(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]] - patient_current_time))
  patient_surv_probs_cumulative <- estimated_surv_probs_updated[[i]]
  
  # Calculate conditional single time point survival probabilities
  patient_surv_probs_single_point <- c(1, diff(patient_surv_probs_cumulative) / head(patient_surv_probs_cumulative, -1))
  patient_surv_probs_single_point
})

# Calculate additional survival time estimates based on conditional single time point survival probabilities
additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional <- data.frame(PatientID = rownames(alive_data))
wa_median_times_updated_conditional <- numeric(nrow(alive_data))
for (i in 1:nrow(alive_data)) {
  start_time_index <- which.min(abs(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]] - alive_data[i, "time"]))
  wa_median_times_updated_conditional[i] <- weighted_avg_time(conditional_surv_probs_single_point[[i]], surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]][start_time_index:length(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]])])
}
additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional$WeightedAverageSurvivalTime <- wa_median_times_updated_conditional - alive_data[["time"]]

# View the updated additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional
additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional

I discovered an index mismatch issue in the code that's causing the error. The issue is that the loop iterating through `alive_data` uses `i` as an index, but the `estimated_surv_probs_updated` list is created with indexes based on the `linear_pred_updated` list. 

We should make the indexes consistent. To fix it, replace the `for` loop that calculates survival probabilities from:

```R
for(i in 1:length(linear_pred_updated)) {
```

To:

```R
for(i in 1:nrow(alive_data)) {
```

Then change the accessing of `linear_pred_updated` from:

```R
lp <- linear_pred_updated[i]
```

To:

```R
lp <- linear_pred_updated[rownames(alive_data)[i]]
```

Now, run the full updated code:

```R
# Load the required package
library(survival)
set.seed(42)

# Generate the sample dataset
nPatients <- 200
status <- rbinom(nPatients, 1, 0.7)
gender <- rbinom(nPatients, 1, 0.5)
age <- rnorm(nPatients, mean = 60, sd = 10)
time <- rexp(nPatients, rate = 0.015)

simData <- data.frame(time = time, status = status, gender = gender, age = age)

# Fit the Cox proportional hazards model
cph_model_updated <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ gender + age, data = simData)

# Get the data for alive patients only
alive_data <- simData[simData[["status"]] == 0, ]

# Calculate linear predictions for alive patients
linear_pred_updated <- predict(cph_model_updated, newdata = alive_data)

length(linear_pred_updated)
# Estimate survival probabilities for alive patients
surv_prob_all_updated <- survfit(cph_model_updated)

summary(surv_prob_all_updated)

estimated_surv_probs_updated <- list()
for(i in 1:nrow(alive_data)) {
  lp <- linear_pred_updated[rownames(alive_data)[i]] 
  patient_current_time <- alive_data[i, "time"]
  patient_times_start_index <- which.min(abs(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]] - patient_current_time))
  patient_surv_probs <- surv_prob_all_updated[["surv"]][patient_times_start_index:length(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]])] * exp(lp)
  estimated_surv_probs_updated[[i]] <- patient_surv_probs
}

weighted_avg_time <- function(surv_probs, surv_times) {
  sum(surv_probs * surv_times) / sum(surv_probs)
}

# Calculate adjusted survival probabilities for each alive patient
conditional_surv_probs_single_point <- lapply(1:nrow(alive_data), function(i) {
  patient_current_time <- alive_data[i, "time"]
  patient_times_start_index <- which.min(abs(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]] - patient_current_time))
  patient_surv_probs_cumulative <- estimated_surv_probs_updated[[i]]
  
  # Calculate conditional single time point survival probabilities
  patient_surv_probs_single_point <- c(1, diff(patient_surv_probs_cumulative) / head(patient_surv_probs_cumulative, -1))
  patient_surv_probs_single_point
})

# Calculate additional survival time estimates based on conditional single time point survival probabilities
additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional <- data.frame(PatientID = rownames(alive_data))
wa_median_times_updated_conditional <- numeric(nrow(alive_data))
for (i in 1:nrow(alive_data)) {
  start_time_index <- which.min(abs(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]] - alive_data[i, "time"]))
  wa_median_times_updated_conditional[i] <- weighted_avg_time(conditional_surv_probs_single_point[[i]], surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]][start_time_index:length(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]])])
}
additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional$WeightedAverageSurvivalTime <- wa_median_times_updated_conditional - alive_data[["time"]]

# View the updated additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional
additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional
```

This code should run without any errors and display the additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional data frame.
why are the additional time saved in additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional so larger than the original time in simData used for fitting the model?

The additional survival times in the `additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional` data frame are larger than the original event times because they represent the estimated remaining survival time for the alive patients based on the current state of the data at the time they were last observed.

In the simulation, alive patients have not yet experienced an event (death), so their current time represents the duration they have survived up to that point. The Cox proportional hazards model is used to estimate the hazard ratios based on the predictor variables (in this case, gender and age). The estimated remaining survival time is then calculated using this model, which gives an idea of how long these patients are expected to survive after their last observed alive time.

Since the model accounts for the patient's covariates (in this case, gender and age), the additional survival time estimates vary depending on the patient's characteristics. Some patients might have higher additional survival times than others based on their gender and age, which are used as predictors in the model.

In summary, the additional survival times are larger than the original event times because they represent the remaining survival time for alive patients, estimated from the point of their last observation using the Cox proportional hazards model.
there are even negative value for additional survival time, that does not make sense. Please revise the code.

You are correct, negative additional survival times do not make sense, as they represent an impossible scenario. The issue arises from the `weighted_avg_time` function, which calculates the weighted average survival times based on the conditional single time point survival probabilities. In some cases, it may result in an additional survival time estimate shorter than a patient's current survival time.

We can address this by modifying the `weighted_avg_time` function with a lower limit for the weighted average, ensuring it is always greater than or equal to the patient's current time. Here is the revised code:

```R
weighted_avg_time_limited <- function(surv_probs, surv_times, min_time) {
  weighted_time <- sum(surv_probs * surv_times) / sum(surv_probs)
  return(max(weighted_time, min_time))
}
```

Now, update the loop that calculates additional survival time estimates using this revised function by adding the `alive_data[i, "time"]` as the lower limit:

Replace the loop

```R
for (i in 1:nrow(alive_data)) {
  start_time_index <- which.min(abs(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]] - alive_data[i, "time"]))
  wa_median_times_updated_conditional[i] <- weighted_avg_time(conditional_surv_probs_single_point[[i]], surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]][start_time_index:length(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]])])
}
```

With the revised loop

```R
for (i in 1:nrow(alive_data)) {
  start_time_index <- which.min(abs(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]] - alive_data[i, "time"]))
  wa_median_times_updated_conditional[i] <- weighted_avg_time_limited(conditional_surv_probs_single_point[[i]], surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]][start_time_index:length(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]])], alive_data[i, "time"])
}
```

Re-run the full code with these modifications, and you should no longer see negative additional survival times. The updated weighted average function ensures that the additional survival time estimate is never shorter than a patient's current survival time.

I don't quite understand the weight process, why only survival probability at a single time point is considered? Shouldn't the cumulative survival probability after the current observed time for these alive patient be considered when calculated the weighted average?

You are right; the cumulative survival probability can also be considered for estimating the additional survival time for alive patients. The previous code considered the conditional single time point survival probabilities when calculating the weighted average, but you can also use the cumulative survival probabilities instead.

To use the cumulative survival probabilities, modify the loop that calculates the additional survival time estimates based on the cumulative survival probabilities.

Replace the loop

```R
for (i in 1:nrow(alive_data)) {
  start_time_index <- which.min(abs(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]] - alive_data[i, "time"]))
  wa_median_times_updated_conditional[i] <- weighted_avg_time_limited(conditional_surv_probs_single_point[[i]], surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]][start_time_index:length(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]])], alive_data[i, "time"])
}
```

With the revised loop

```R
for (i in 1:nrow(alive_data)) {
  start_time_index <- which.min(abs(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]] - alive_data[i, "time"]))
  wa_median_times_updated_conditional[i] <- weighted_avg_time_limited(estimated_surv_probs_updated[[i]], surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]][start_time_index:length(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]])], alive_data[i, "time"])
}
```

Re-run the full code with these modifications, and you will be using the cumulative survival probabilities instead of the conditional single time point survival probabilities when calculating the weighted average additional survival times for alive patients. This change will result in predictions that consider the cumulative survival probabilities after the current observed time, as you suggested.

the cumulative process should start after current time which has already been observed, for example, for patient A, the observed time till when the patient is still alive is 40 months, the next point is 41, and the survival probability for this single time point 95%, and the next time point is 45 months, with survival probability for this single time point is 85%, then the cumulative probability is 95% * 85%. That means, the cumulative process should start after time already observed. Please revise accordingly

I understand your point now, and you are right. The cumulative probabilities should be considered after the current observed time, and we need to revise the code accordingly to do that. 

Modify the loop that calculates the additional survival time estimates based on the cumulative survival probabilities. First, create a function to calculate the product of survival probabilities after the current observed time:

```R
cumulative_surv_prob_after_current <- function(surv_probs_single_point, current_time, surv_times) {
  start_index <- which.min(abs(surv_times - current_time))
  cum_prob <- prod(surv_probs_single_point[start_index:length(surv_probs_single_point)])
  return(cum_prob)
}
```

Then, revise the loop that calculates the additional survival time estimates. Replace the previous loop with the following loop:

```R
for (i in 1:nrow(alive_data)) {
  start_time_index <- which.min(abs(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]] - alive_data[i, "time"]))
  patient_surv_probs_cumulative <- estimated_surv_probs_updated[[i]]
  patient_surv_probs_single_point <- c(1, diff(patient_surv_probs_cumulative) / head(patient_surv_probs_cumulative, -1))
  
  cum_prob <- cumulative_surv_prob_after_current(patient_surv_probs_single_point, alive_data[i, "time"], surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]][start_time_index:length(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]])])
  
  wa_median_times_updated_conditional[i] <- weighted_avg_time_limited(cum_prob, surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]][start_time_index:length(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]])], alive_data[i, "time"])
}
```

Make sure you have both the `weighted_avg_time_limited()` function and the `cumulative_surv_prob_after_current()` function defined before running the modified loop.

With these changes, the code will now consider the cumulative probabilities of the single time points after the current observed time when calculating the weighted average additional survival times for alive patients. This will provide a more accurate estimate of additional survival time based on the observed data.
can you please provide the full code?

Certainly! Here's the full revised code, along with the modifications to consider the cumulative probabilities after the current observed time:

```R
# Load the required package
library(survival)
set.seed(42)

# Generate the sample dataset
nPatients <- 200
status <- rbinom(nPatients, 1, 0.7)
gender <- rbinom(nPatients, 1, 0.5)
age <- rnorm(nPatients, mean = 60, sd = 10)
time <- rexp(nPatients, rate = 0.015)

simData <- data.frame(time = time, status = status, gender = gender, age = age)

# Fit the Cox proportional hazards model
cph_model_updated <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ gender + age, data = simData)

# Get the data for alive patients only
alive_data <- simData[simData[["status"]] == 0, ]

# Calculate linear predictions for alive patients
linear_pred_updated <- predict(cph_model_updated, newdata = alive_data)

length(linear_pred_updated)
# Estimate survival probabilities for alive patients
surv_prob_all_updated <- survfit(cph_model_updated)

summary(surv_prob_all_updated)

estimated_surv_probs_updated <- list()
for (i in 1:nrow(alive_data)) {
  lp <- linear_pred_updated[rownames(alive_data)[i]] 
  patient_current_time <- alive_data[i, "time"]
  patient_times_start_index <- which.min(abs(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]] - patient_current_time))
  patient_surv_probs <- surv_prob_all_updated[["surv"]][patient_times_start_index:length(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]])] * exp(lp)
  estimated_surv_probs_updated[[i]] <- patient_surv_probs
}

weighted_avg_time_limited <- function(surv_probs, surv_times, min_time) {
  weighted_time <- sum(surv_probs * surv_times) / sum(surv_probs)
  return(max(weighted_time, min_time))
}

cumulative_surv_prob_after_current <- function(surv_probs_single_point, current_time, surv_times) {
  start_index <- which.min(abs(surv_times - current_time))
  cum_prob <- prod(surv_probs_single_point[start_index:length(surv_probs_single_point)])
  return(cum_prob)
}

# Calculate additional survival time estimates based on the cumulative survival probabilities after the current observed time
additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional <- data.frame(PatientID = rownames(alive_data))
wa_median_times_updated_conditional <- numeric(nrow(alive_data))
for (i in 1:nrow(alive_data)) {
  start_time_index <- which.min(abs(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]] - alive_data[i, "time"]))
  patient_surv_probs_cumulative <- estimated_surv_probs_updated[[i]]
  patient_surv_probs_single_point <- c(1, diff(patient_surv_probs_cumulative) / head(patient_surv_probs_cumulative, -1))
  
  cum_prob <- cumulative_surv_prob_after_current(patient_surv_probs_single_point, alive_data[i, "time"], surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]][start_time_index:length(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]])])
  
  wa_median_times_updated_conditional[i] <- weighted_avg_time_limited(cum_prob, surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]][start_time_index:length(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]])], alive_data[i, "time"])
}
additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional$WeightedAverageSurvivalTime <- wa_median_times_updated_conditional - alive_data[["time"]]

# View the updated additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional
additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional
```

Now, the code calculates the additional survival time estimates using the cumulative survival probabilities after the current observed time for alive patients. This should provide more accurate results.
the values returned for additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional are all 'NaN', please revise.

Apologies for the confusion. After revisiting the code, I discovered that the `cumulative_surv_prob_after_current` function is causing the issue because the logic of calculating the `cum_prob` needs revision. 

Here's the revised function:

```R
cumulative_surv_prob_after_current <- function(surv_probs_single_point, current_time, surv_times) {
  start_index <- which.min(abs(surv_times - current_time))
  cum_prob <- c(1, surv_probs_single_point[(start_index + 1):length(surv_probs_single_point)])
  cum_prob <- cumprod(cum_prob)
  return(cum_prob)
}
```

Now, replace the existing `cumulative_surv_prob_after_current` function in the full code and rerun the code. This should resolve the issue and not return 'NaN' values for additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional.

Here's the revised full code:

```R
# Load the required package
library(survival)
set.seed(42)

# Generate the sample dataset
nPatients <- 200
status <- rbinom(nPatients, 1, 0.7)
gender <- rbinom(nPatients, 1, 0.5)
age <- rnorm(nPatients, mean = 60, sd = 10)
time <- rexp(nPatients, rate = 0.015)

simData <- data.frame(time = time, status = status, gender = gender, age = age)

# Fit the Cox proportional hazards model
cph_model_updated <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ gender + age, data = simData)

# Get the data for alive patients only
alive_data <- simData[simData[["status"]] == 0, ]

# Calculate linear predictions for alive patients
linear_pred_updated <- predict(cph_model_updated, newdata = alive_data)

length(linear_pred_updated)
# Estimate survival probabilities for alive patients
surv_prob_all_updated <- survfit(cph_model_updated)

summary(surv_prob_all_updated)

estimated_surv_probs_updated <- list()
for (i in 1:nrow(alive_data)) {
  lp <- linear_pred_updated[rownames(alive_data)[i]]
  patient_current_time <- alive_data[i, "time"]
  patient_times_start_index <- which.min(abs(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]] - patient_current_time))
  patient_surv_probs <- surv_prob_all_updated[["surv"]][patient_times_start_index:length(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]])] * exp(lp)
  estimated_surv_probs_updated[[i]] <- patient_surv_probs
}

weighted_avg_time_limited <- function(surv_probs, surv_times, min_time) {
  weighted_time <- sum(surv_probs * surv_times) / sum(surv_probs)
  return(max(weighted_time, min_time))
}

cumulative_surv_prob_after_current <- function(surv_probs_single_point, current_time, surv_times) {
  start_index <- which.min(abs(surv_times - current_time))
  cum_prob <- c(1, surv_probs_single_point[(start_index + 1):length(surv_probs_single_point)])
  cum_prob <- cumprod(cum_prob)
  return(cum_prob)
}

# Calculate additional survival time estimates based on the cumulative survival probabilities after the current observed time
additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional <- data.frame(PatientID = rownames(alive_data))
wa_median_times_updated_conditional <- numeric(nrow(alive_data))
for (i in 1:nrow(alive_data)) {
  start_time_index <- which.min(abs(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]] - alive_data[i, "time"]))
  patient_surv_probs_cumulative <- estimated_surv_probs_updated[[i]]
  patient_surv_probs_single_point <- c(1, diff(patient_surv_probs_cumulative) / head(patient_surv_probs_cumulative, -1))
  
  cum_prob <- cumulative_surv_prob_after_current(patient_surv_probs_single_point, alive_data[i, "time"], surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]][start_time_index:length(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]])])
  
  wa_median_times_updated_conditional[i] <- weighted_avg_time_limited(cum_prob, surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]][start_time_index:length(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]])], alive_data[i, "time"])
}
additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional$WeightedAverageSurvivalTime <- wa_median_times_updated_conditional - alive_data[["time"]]

# View the updated additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional
additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional
```

```R
weighted_avg_time_limited <- function(surv_probs, surv_times, min_time) {
  weighted_time <- sum(surv_probs * surv_times) / sum(surv_probs) + min_time
  return(weighted_time)
}
```

```R
# Load the required package
library(survival)
set.seed(42)

# Generate the sample dataset
nPatients <- 200
status <- rbinom(nPatients, 1, 0.7)
gender <- rbinom(nPatients, 1, 0.5)
age <- rnorm(nPatients, mean = 60, sd = 10)
time <- rexp(nPatients, rate = 0.015)

simData <- data.frame(time = time, status = status, gender = gender, age = age)

# Fit the Cox proportional hazards model
cph_model_updated <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ gender + age, data = simData)

# Get the data for alive patients only
alive_data <- simData[simData[["status"]] == 0, ]

# Calculate linear predictions for alive patients
linear_pred_updated <- predict(cph_model_updated, newdata = alive_data)

length(linear_pred_updated)
# Estimate survival probabilities for alive patients
surv_prob_all_updated <- survfit(cph_model_updated)

summary(surv_prob_all_updated)

estimated_surv_probs_updated <- list()
for (i in 1:nrow(alive_data)) {
  lp <- linear_pred_updated[rownames(alive_data)[i]]
  patient_current_time <- alive_data[i, "time"]
  patient_times_start_index <- which.min(abs(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]] - patient_current_time))
  patient_surv_probs <- surv_prob_all_updated[["surv"]][patient_times_start_index:length(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]])] * exp(lp)
  estimated_surv_probs_updated[[i]] <- patient_surv_probs
}

weighted_avg_time_limited <- function(surv_probs, surv_times, min_time) {
  weighted_time <- sum(surv_probs * surv_times) / sum(surv_probs) + min_time
  return(weighted_time)
}

# Calculate additional survival time estimates based on the cumulative survival probabilities after the current observed time
additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional <- data.frame(PatientID = rownames(alive_data))
wa_median_times_updated_conditional <- numeric(nrow(alive_data))
for (i in 1:nrow(alive_data)) {
  start_time_index <- which.min(abs(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]] - alive_data[i, "time"]))
  patient_surv_probs_cumulative <- estimated_surv_probs_updated[[i]]
  patient_surv_probs_single_point <- c(1, diff(patient_surv_probs_cumulative) / head(patient_surv_probs_cumulative, -1))
  
  future_surv_single_points <- patient_surv_probs_single_point[(start_time_index + 1):length(patient_surv_probs_single_point)]
  future_surv_times <- surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]][(start_time_index + 1):length(surv_prob_all_updated[["time"]])]
  
  wa_median_times_updated_conditional[i] <- weighted_avg_time_limited(future_surv_single_points, future_surv_times, alive_data[i, "time"])
}
additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional$WeightedAverageSurvivalTime <- wa_median_times_updated_conditional - alive_data[["time"]]

# View the updated additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional
additional_survival_time_updated_wa_conditional
```
